notes: User cancels a long in-flight aider turn with Ctrl-C, then issues a recovery prompt that proves the parser reset. The scenario's user-observable signal is ready→working→ready with state NOT sticking in working post-interrupt, plus a clean recovery arc on the same per-cwd session. AIDER-SPECIFIC structural nuance (see assessment.json): aider is the lone FilesUnderCWD adapter — its transcript is markdown (.aider.chat.history.md) parsed by a RawParser. A Ctrl-C-cancelled turn writes NO `> Tokens:` line, so the parser emits no turn-closing assistant_message; the turn stays open. Unlike codex (no settle) and pi (settles only on the recovery turn's stopReason=stop), aider settles the interrupted turn FROM IDLE ALONE: IdleFlush synthesizes a bare turn_done once the transcript has been quiet for aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms), which IsAgentDone() recognizes and the classifier's 'agent finished turn → ready' rule settles working→ready — independent of any abort marker and independent of the recovery prompt arriving. The effective post-interrupt settle latency is ~max(1500ms IdleFlush floor, the daemon's 5s SessionDetector staleWorking refresh tick), so interrupt_settle tolerates that window rather than demanding an immediate flip. The recovery prompt's `#### ` line then resets the parser's assistantBuffer and re-opens a turn (the parser comment names this exact interrupted-then-recovered case), and the recovery turn runs a normal ready→working→ready arc, proving the reset. irrlicht CANNOT detect the interrupt itself — there is no abort marker in the markdown and the parser has no interrupt-marker handling; the post-interrupt ready is produced by IdleFlush, not by observing the Ctrl-C. Spec compliance is unaffected: the observable arc and the no-stick invariant both hold. SESSION-IDENTITY (same workaround as aider/multi-turn-conversation): aider's transcript is lazy, so at launch the process scanner emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready — session_birth, which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch', legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARCS must land on the per-cwd rollout session: t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); every later phase, the pid_bind, and the teardown then pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. The driver's interrupt step sends tmux Ctrl-C and is NOT followed by wait_turn (the cancelled turn yields no `> Tokens:` line and would hang wait_turn); the recipe sleeps after the interrupt, then sends the recovery prompt and waits on THAT turn, then sleeps again so IdleFlush + the 5s staleWorking tick land the recovery settle before teardown. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording (the cancelled essay text), per-turn token counts, how much of the essay streamed before Ctrl-C landed, and sub-second timing all vary (model-output variance); the cancelled turn contributes zero tokens/cost (no `> Tokens:` line was written). Stable structurally: a ready→working→ready arc on one rollout session_id that does NOT stick in working post-interrupt and never enters waiting, a second clean ready→working→ready recovery arc on the same session, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (aider's transcript is lazy, so this is the launch-time proc-<PID> pre-session)
First prompt (long essay) → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
PID bound to the rollout session

After the Ctrl-C interrupt the cancelled turn writes no turn-done marker; IdleFlush settles the rollout session working→ready from transcript-quiet alone — state does not stick in working, and never entered waiting
Recovery prompt re-opens a turn → working on the same rollout session (proving the parser reset after the interrupt)
The recovery turn completes and settles the rollout session back to ready — a clean ready→working→ready arc, never entering waiting
REPL torn down once after the recovery turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
